title: Analytics - Device Deleted
scenario: Firebase "device_deleted" analytics event fails to log device-specific fields (Device Name, PID, Serial) when a device is removed from the Poly Studio Mobile app
steps: 1. Open the Poly Studio Mobile app on the Android device.
2. Navigate to Home screen and ensure a Poly Bluetooth audio device is connected and shown.
3. Open the Firebase console and navigate to the Analytics page for the "device_deleted" event.
4. Verify the page loads correctly and displays all default elements: graph, events in last 30 min, event count by country/gender, events per session, app version, app build, app environment.
5. Verify the page also displays device-specific fields: Device Name, Device PID, Device Serial.
6. Return to the Poly Studio Mobile app.
7. Remove/delete the device from the app (e.g., go to Device Settings → [Device Name] → Remove Device or similar).
8. Also remove the device from the phone's Bluetooth settings (Settings → Bluetooth → Forget/Pairing device).
9. Wait for some time (typically a few hours) for Firebase event processing.
10. Return to the Firebase console and check the "device_deleted" event page again.
11. Verify that the event count for the related elements (default elements and device-specific fields) has increased.
expected: The Firebase Analytics page for the "device_deleted" event loads correctly with all default elements (graph, events in last 30 min, event count by country/gender, events per session, app version, app build, app environment) and device-specific fields (Device Name, Device PID, Device Serial). After removing the device from the app and phone Bluetooth settings, the event count for these elements increases after a few hours.
